General rogue wave solutions to the discrete nonlinear Schrödinger equation

Yasuhiro Ohta
Department of Mathematics, Kobe University
Rokko, Kobe 657-8501, Japan
   Bao-Feng Feng
School of Mathematical and Statistical Sciences
The University of Texas Rio Grande Valley
Abstract

In the present paper, we attempt to construct both the general rogue wave solutions to the fully discrete nonlinear Schrödinger (fd-NLS) equation via the KP-Toda reduction method. First, we deduce the general breather solution of the fd-NLS equation starting from a pair of bilinear equations. We then derive the general rogue wave solution by taking a limit to the breather solution.

1 Introduction

Rogue waves (RWs) or freak waves are spontaneously excited local nonlinear waves with large amplitudes which appear from nowhere and disappear with no trace [1]. The simplest form of such waves were firstly discovered Peregrine in the nonlinear Schrödinger (NLS) equation [2], and their higher order forms were found 20 years later in [3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9]. Such extreme wave have been observed in various different contexts such as oceanography [10], hydrodynamic [11, 12], Bose-Einstein condensate [13], plasma [14] and nonlinear optic [11, 15, 16].

Motivated by these physical applications, rogue wave solutions have been found in many other nonlinear wave equations such as the derivative Schrödinger (NLS) equation [17, 18, 19, 20, 21], the Manakov system [22, 23, 24], Davey-Stewartson I and II equation [25, 26], the three-wave equation [27], the Boussinesq equation [28], the Yajima-Oikawa equation [29, 30],.

On the other hand, the study of rogue waves in discrete integrable systems is much less. As far as we are aware, only the rogue waves in semi-discrete NLS equation, or the so-called Ablowitz-Ladik equation have been reported in the literature [31, 32]. In the present paper, we attempt to construct the rogue wave solutions to the fully discrete nonlinear Schrödinger (fd-NLS) equation, which was originally discovered by Ablowitz and Ladik [33, 34] and rediscovered by Hirota and Ohta through Hirota’s bilinear approach [35]. It was also discussed in [36, 37]. In a recent paper by Hirose et al., the integrable discretization of the local induction equation which is gauge equivalent to the NLS equation was presented in [38].

The remainder of the present paper is organized as follows. In section 2, we will construct general breather solution to the fd-NLS equation. We derive general rogue wave solutions to the fd-NLS equation by taking the limit to the general breather solution in section 3. The paper is concluded in section 4.

2 Breather solution for fully discrete NLS equation

Based on the bilinear formulation, we are able to derive the breather solution of the fully discrete NLS equation, which is given by the following theorem.

Theorem 1.

The fully discrete NLS equation,

{i​(qkt+1−qkt)=(qk+1t+qk−1t+1)​(1+ϵ​|qkt|2)​Γkt,Γk+1t=1+ϵ​|qkt|21+ϵ​|qkt+1|2​Γkt,casesisuperscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘1𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘1𝑡11italic-ϵsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘𝑡2superscriptsubscriptΓ𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscriptΓ𝑘1𝑡1italic-ϵsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘𝑡21italic-ϵsuperscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘𝑡12superscriptsubscriptΓ𝑘𝑡\left\{\begin{array}[]{l}\mathrm{i}(q_{k}^{t+1}-q_{k}^{t})=(q_{k+1}^{t}+q_{k-1}^{t+1})(1+\epsilon|q_{k}^{t}|^{2})\Gamma_{k}^{t},\\[5.0pt] \displaystyle\Gamma_{k+1}^{t}=\frac{1+\epsilon|q_{k}^{t}|^{2}}{1+\epsilon|q_{k}^{t+1}|^{2}}\Gamma_{k}^{t},\end{array}\right. (1)

where ϵ=±1italic-ϵplus-or-minus1\epsilon=\pm 1, admits the N𝑁N-breather solution,

qkt=gktfkt​1−r2​|r|​ei​k​θ​(1−c1−c¯​1+r​c¯1+r​c)t,Γkt=4​r​R(1+r)2​fk−1t+1​fktfkt+1​fk−1t,formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscript𝑞𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡1𝑟2𝑟superscript𝑒i𝑘𝜃superscript1𝑐1¯𝑐1𝑟¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐𝑡superscriptsubscriptΓ𝑘𝑡4𝑟𝑅superscript1𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘1𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘1𝑡q_{k}^{t}=\frac{g_{k}^{t}}{f_{k}^{t}}\frac{1-r}{2\sqrt{|r|}}e^{\mathrm{i}k\theta}\left(\frac{1-c}{1-\bar{c}}\frac{1+r\bar{c}}{1+rc}\right)^{t},\quad\Gamma_{k}^{t}=\frac{4rR}{(1+r)^{2}}\frac{f_{k-1}^{t+1}f_{k}^{t}}{f_{k}^{t+1}f_{k-1}^{t}}, (2)

where ¯¯absent\bar{\ } means complex conjugate, r𝑟r is a real constant whose sign coincides with ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon, ϵ=sign​ritalic-ϵsign𝑟\epsilon=\hbox{sign}\,r, c𝑐c is a complex constant, R𝑅R and θ𝜃\theta are determined by

1i​1+c1−c¯​1−r​c¯1+r​c=R​ei​θ,1i1𝑐1¯𝑐1𝑟¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐𝑅superscript𝑒i𝜃\frac{1}{\mathrm{i}}\frac{1+c}{1-\bar{c}}\frac{1-r\bar{c}}{1+rc}=Re^{\mathrm{i}\theta}, (3)

with

τkt​(n)=det1≤i,j≤N(Ai​j(n)​(k,t)),superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛subscriptformulae-sequence1𝑖𝑗𝑁superscriptsubscript𝐴𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝑡\tau_{k}^{t}(n)=\det_{1\leq i,j\leq N}\left(A_{ij}^{(n)}(k,t)\right), (5)
Ai​j(n)=11−r​pi​p¯j​(ai​φn​(pi)​aj​φ−n​(pj)¯+bi​φn​(−pi)​bj​φ−n​(−pj)¯)superscriptsubscript𝐴𝑖𝑗𝑛11𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖¯subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖¯subscript𝑏𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗\displaystyle A_{ij}^{(n)}=\frac{1}{1-rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}\left(a_{i}\varphi_{n}(p_{i})\overline{a_{j}\varphi_{-n}(p_{j})}+b_{i}\varphi_{n}(-p_{i})\overline{b_{j}\varphi_{-n}(-p_{j})}\right)
−11+r​pi​p¯j​(ai​φn​(pi)​bj​φ−n​(−pj)¯+bi​φn​(−pi)​aj​φ−n​(pj)¯),11𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖¯subscript𝑏𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖¯subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗\displaystyle\qquad-\frac{1}{1+rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}\left(a_{i}\varphi_{n}(p_{i})\overline{b_{j}\varphi_{-n}(-p_{j})}+b_{i}\varphi_{n}(-p_{i})\overline{a_{j}\varphi_{-n}(p_{j})}\right), (6)
φn​(p)=(1−r​p1+p)n​(1+p1+r​p)k​(1+p1+r​p​1+p/c¯1+r​c​p)t,subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝superscript1𝑟𝑝1𝑝𝑛superscript1𝑝1𝑟𝑝𝑘superscript1𝑝1𝑟𝑝1𝑝¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐𝑝𝑡\varphi_{n}(p)=\left(\frac{1-rp}{1+p}\right)^{n}\left(\frac{1+p}{1+rp}\right)^{k}\left(\frac{1+p}{1+rp}\frac{1+p/\bar{c}}{1+rcp}\right)^{t}, (7)

where pisubscript𝑝𝑖p_{i}, aisubscript𝑎𝑖a_{i}, bisubscript𝑏𝑖b_{i} are complex constants.

Proof.

By using the Gramian technique, we can directly prove that the τ𝜏\tau function of discrete KP/Toda hierarchy,

τn​(k,K,l,L)=det1≤i,j≤N(mi​j(n)​(k,K,l,L)),subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿subscriptformulae-sequence1𝑖𝑗𝑁superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\tau_{n}(k,K,l,L)=\det_{1\leq i,j\leq N}\left(m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)\right),

satisfies the discrete bilinear equations,

{(1−a​A)​τn​(k+1,K+1,l,L)​τn​(k,K,l,L)−τn​(k+1,K,l,L)​τn​(k,K+1,l,L)+a​A​τn+1​(k+1,K,l,L)​τn−1​(k,K+1,l,L)=0,A​(a−b)​(1−a​B)​τn+1​(k+1,K,l+1,L+1)​τn​(k,K+1,l,L)−a​(A−B)​(1−A​b)​τn+1​(k+1,K,l,L)​τn​(k,K+1,l+1,L+1)+A​b​(1−a​A)​τn+1​(k,K,l+1,L+1)​τn​(k+1,K+1,l,L)−a​B​(1−a​A)​τn+1​(k+1,K+1,l,L)​τn​(k,K,l+1,L+1)=0,cases1𝑎𝐴subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘1𝐾1𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘1𝐾𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾1𝑙𝐿𝑎𝐴subscript𝜏𝑛1𝑘1𝐾𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛1𝑘𝐾1𝑙𝐿0𝐴𝑎𝑏1𝑎𝐵subscript𝜏𝑛1𝑘1𝐾𝑙1𝐿1subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾1𝑙𝐿𝑎𝐴𝐵1𝐴𝑏subscript𝜏𝑛1𝑘1𝐾𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾1𝑙1𝐿1𝐴𝑏1𝑎𝐴subscript𝜏𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙1𝐿1subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘1𝐾1𝑙𝐿𝑎𝐵1𝑎𝐴subscript𝜏𝑛1𝑘1𝐾1𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙1𝐿10\left\{\begin{array}[]{l}(1-aA)\tau_{n}(k+1,K+1,l,L)\tau_{n}(k,K,l,L)\\[5.0pt] \quad-\tau_{n}(k+1,K,l,L)\tau_{n}(k,K+1,l,L)\\[5.0pt] \quad+aA\tau_{n+1}(k+1,K,l,L)\tau_{n-1}(k,K+1,l,L)=0,\\[5.0pt] A(a-b)(1-aB)\tau_{n+1}(k+1,K,l+1,L+1)\tau_{n}(k,K+1,l,L)\\[5.0pt] \quad-a(A-B)(1-Ab)\tau_{n+1}(k+1,K,l,L)\tau_{n}(k,K+1,l+1,L+1)\\[5.0pt] \quad+Ab(1-aA)\tau_{n+1}(k,K,l+1,L+1)\tau_{n}(k+1,K+1,l,L)\\[5.0pt] \quad-aB(1-aA)\tau_{n+1}(k+1,K+1,l,L)\tau_{n}(k,K,l+1,L+1)=0,\end{array}\right. (8)

if the matrix element mi​j(n)superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛m_{ij}^{(n)} satisfies

mi​j(n)​(k,K+1,l,L)−mi​j(n)​(k,K,l,L)=(−1)n​A​ϕi(n−1)​(k,K+1,l,L)​ψj(−n−1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾1𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscript1𝑛𝐴superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛1𝑘𝐾1𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K+1,l,L)-m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=(-1)^{n}A\phi_{i}^{(n-1)}(k,K+1,l,L)\psi_{j}^{(-n-1)}(k,K,l,L),
mi​j(n)​(k,K,l+1,L)−mi​j(n)​(k,K,l,L)=(−1)n​b​ϕi(n)​(k,K,l+1,L)​ψj(−n)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscript1𝑛𝑏superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K,l+1,L)-m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=(-1)^{n}b\phi_{i}^{(n)}(k,K,l+1,L)\psi_{j}^{(-n)}(k,K,l,L),

where ϕi(n)superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛\phi_{i}^{(n)} and ψj(n)superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛\psi_{j}^{(n)} are arbitrary functions satisfying the linear dispersion relations,

ϕi(n)​(k,K,l,L)−ϕi(n)​(k−1,K,l,L)=a​ϕi(n+1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛𝑘1𝐾𝑙𝐿𝑎superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\phi_{i}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)-\phi_{i}^{(n)}(k-1,K,l,L)=a\phi_{i}^{(n+1)}(k,K,l,L),

ϕi(n)​(k,K,l,L)−ϕi(n)​(k,K,l,L−1)=B​ϕi(n−1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿1𝐵superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\phi_{i}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)-\phi_{i}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L-1)=B\phi_{i}^{(n-1)}(k,K,l,L),
ψj(n)​(k+1,K,l,L)−ψj(n)​(k,K,l,L)=a​ψj(n+1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘1𝐾𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿𝑎superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k+1,K,l,L)-\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=a\psi_{j}^{(n+1)}(k,K,l,L),
ψj(n)​(k,K+1,l,L)−ψj(n)​(k,K,l,L)=A​ψj(n−1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾1𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿𝐴superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K+1,l,L)-\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=A\psi_{j}^{(n-1)}(k,K,l,L),
ψj(n)​(k,K,l+1,L)−ψj(n)​(k,K,l,L)=b​ψj(n+1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙1𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿𝑏superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l+1,L)-\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=b\psi_{j}^{(n+1)}(k,K,l,L),
ψj(n)​(k,K,l,L+1)−ψj(n)​(k,K,l,L)=B​ψj(n−1)​(k,K,l,L),superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿1superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿𝐵superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛1𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L+1)-\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=B\psi_{j}^{(n-1)}(k,K,l,L),

where k𝑘k, K𝐾K, l𝑙l, L𝐿L are discrete independent variables and a𝑎a, A𝐴A, b𝑏b, B𝐵B are difference intervals. In order to derive the breather solution, we assume

mi​j(n)​(k,K,l,L)superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)
=∑ν=12∑μ=12ai​ν​bj​μpi​ν+qj​μ​(−pi​νqj​μ)n​(1+a​qj​μ1−a​pi​ν)k​(1+A/qj​μ1−A/pi​ν)K​(1+b​qj​μ1−b​pi​ν)l​(1+B/qj​μ1−B/pi​ν)L,absentsuperscriptsubscript𝜈12superscriptsubscript𝜇12subscript𝑎𝑖𝜈subscript𝑏𝑗𝜇subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖𝜈subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇𝑛superscript1𝑎subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇1𝑎subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝑘superscript1𝐴subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇1𝐴subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝐾superscript1𝑏subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇1𝑏subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝑙superscript1𝐵subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇1𝐵subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝐿\displaystyle\quad=\sum_{\nu=1}^{2}\sum_{\mu=1}^{2}\frac{a_{i\nu}b_{j\mu}}{p_{i\nu}+q_{j\mu}}\left(-\frac{p_{i\nu}}{q_{j\mu}}\right)^{n}\left(\frac{1+aq_{j\mu}}{1-ap_{i\nu}}\right)^{k}\left(\frac{1+A/q_{j\mu}}{1-A/p_{i\nu}}\right)^{K}\left(\frac{1+bq_{j\mu}}{1-bp_{i\nu}}\right)^{l}\left(\frac{1+B/q_{j\mu}}{1-B/p_{i\nu}}\right)^{L},
ϕi(n)​(k,K,l,L)=∑ν=12ai​ν​pi​νn​(1−a​pi​ν)−k​(1−A/pi​ν)−K​(1−b​pi​ν)−l​(1−B/pi​ν)−L,superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜈12subscript𝑎𝑖𝜈superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝑛superscript1𝑎subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝑘superscript1𝐴subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝐾superscript1𝑏subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝑙superscript1𝐵subscript𝑝𝑖𝜈𝐿\displaystyle\phi_{i}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=\sum_{\nu=1}^{2}a_{i\nu}p_{i\nu}^{n}(1-ap_{i\nu})^{-k}(1-A/p_{i\nu})^{-K}(1-bp_{i\nu})^{-l}(1-B/p_{i\nu})^{-L},
ψj(n)​(k,K,l,L)=∑μ=12bj​μ​qj​μn​(1+a​qj​μ)k​(1+A/qj​μ)K​(1+b​qj​μ)l​(1+B/qj​μ)L,superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscript𝜇12subscript𝑏𝑗𝜇superscriptsubscript𝑞𝑗𝜇𝑛superscript1𝑎subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇𝑘superscript1𝐴subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇𝐾superscript1𝑏subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇𝑙superscript1𝐵subscript𝑞𝑗𝜇𝐿\displaystyle\psi_{j}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L)=\sum_{\mu=1}^{2}b_{j\mu}q_{j\mu}^{n}(1+aq_{j\mu})^{k}(1+A/q_{j\mu})^{K}(1+bq_{j\mu})^{l}(1+B/q_{j\mu})^{L},

where pi​νsubscript𝑝𝑖𝜈p_{i\nu}, qj​μsubscript𝑞𝑗𝜇q_{j\mu}, ai​νsubscript𝑎𝑖𝜈a_{i\nu}, bj​μsubscript𝑏𝑗𝜇b_{j\mu} are arbitrary constants. Obviously, the ϕi(n)superscriptsubscriptitalic-ϕ𝑖𝑛\phi_{i}^{(n)} and ψj(n)superscriptsubscript𝜓𝑗𝑛\psi_{j}^{(n)} defined satisfy the above linear dispersion relations, therefore the determinant with above defined element mi​j(n)​(k,K,l,L)superscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿m_{ij}^{(n)}(k,K,l,L) satisfy the bilinear equations (8).

Next, we proceed to the reductions. By imposing

pi​2=−Aa​1−a​pi​1pi​1−A,qj​2=−Aa​1+a​qj​1qj​1+A,formulae-sequencesubscript𝑝𝑖2𝐴𝑎1𝑎subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝐴subscript𝑞𝑗2𝐴𝑎1𝑎subscript𝑞𝑗1subscript𝑞𝑗1𝐴p_{i2}=-\frac{A}{a}\frac{1-ap_{i1}}{p_{i1}-A},\quad q_{j2}=-\frac{A}{a}\frac{1+aq_{j1}}{q_{j1}+A},

we can show that τnsubscript𝜏𝑛\tau_{n} satisfies the following reduction condition,

τn​(k+1,K−1,l,L)=τn​(k,K,l,L)​∏i=1Nqi​1​qi​2pi​1​pi​2,subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘1𝐾1𝑙𝐿subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑖1𝑁subscript𝑞𝑖1subscript𝑞𝑖2subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖2\tau_{n}(k+1,K-1,l,L)=\tau_{n}(k,K,l,L)\prod_{i=1}^{N}\frac{q_{i1}q_{i2}}{p_{i1}p_{i2}},

it follows that the bilinear equations (8) are reduced to

{(1−a​A)​σk+1t​(n)​σk−1t​(n)−σkt​(n)​σkt​(n)+a​A​σkt​(n+1)​σkt​(n−1)=0,A​(a−b)​(1−a​B)​σkt+1​(n+1)​σkt​(n)−a​(A−B)​(1−A​b)​σkt​(n+1)​σkt+1​(n)+A​b​(1−a​A)​σk−1t+1​(n+1)​σk+1t​(n)−a​B​(1−a​A)​σk+1t​(n+1)​σk−1t+1​(n)=0,cases1𝑎𝐴superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘1𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘1𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛𝑎𝐴superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛10𝐴𝑎𝑏1𝑎𝐵superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡1𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛𝑎𝐴𝐵1𝐴𝑏superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡1𝑛𝐴𝑏1𝑎𝐴superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘1𝑡1𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘1𝑡𝑛𝑎𝐵1𝑎𝐴superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘1𝑡𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘1𝑡1𝑛0\left\{\begin{array}[]{l}(1-aA)\sigma_{k+1}^{t}(n)\sigma_{k-1}^{t}(n)-\sigma_{k}^{t}(n)\sigma_{k}^{t}(n)+aA\sigma_{k}^{t}(n+1)\sigma_{k}^{t}(n-1)=0,\\[5.0pt] A(a-b)(1-aB)\sigma_{k}^{t+1}(n+1)\sigma_{k}^{t}(n)-a(A-B)(1-Ab)\sigma_{k}^{t}(n+1)\sigma_{k}^{t+1}(n)\\[5.0pt] \quad+Ab(1-aA)\sigma_{k-1}^{t+1}(n+1)\sigma_{k+1}^{t}(n)-aB(1-aA)\sigma_{k+1}^{t}(n+1)\sigma_{k-1}^{t+1}(n)=0,\end{array}\right. (9)

by taking σkt​(n)=τn​(k,0,t,t)superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛subscript𝜏𝑛𝑘0𝑡𝑡\sigma_{k}^{t}(n)=\tau_{n}(k,0,t,t). Furthermore by taking

A=−ϵ​a¯,B=−ϵ​b¯,qj​1=a¯a​p¯j​1+ϵ​a1−a¯​p¯j​1,bj​1=a¯j​1a​(1−a¯​p¯j​1),bj​2=a¯j​2a​(1−a¯​p¯j​2),formulae-sequence𝐴italic-ϵ¯𝑎formulae-sequence𝐵italic-ϵ¯𝑏formulae-sequencesubscript𝑞𝑗1¯𝑎𝑎subscript¯𝑝𝑗1italic-ϵ𝑎1¯𝑎subscript¯𝑝𝑗1formulae-sequencesubscript𝑏𝑗1subscript¯𝑎𝑗1𝑎1¯𝑎subscript¯𝑝𝑗1subscript𝑏𝑗2subscript¯𝑎𝑗2𝑎1¯𝑎subscript¯𝑝𝑗2A=-\epsilon\bar{a},\quad B=-\epsilon\bar{b},\quad q_{j1}=\frac{\bar{a}}{a}\frac{\bar{p}_{j1}+\epsilon a}{1-\bar{a}\bar{p}_{j1}},\quad b_{j1}=\frac{\bar{a}_{j1}}{a(1-\bar{a}\bar{p}_{j1})},\quad b_{j2}=\frac{\bar{a}_{j2}}{a(1-\bar{a}\bar{p}_{j2})},

where ϵ=+1italic-ϵ1\epsilon=+1 or −11-1, σkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛\sigma_{k}^{t}(n) satisfies the complex conjugate condition,

σkt​(−n)​G=σkt​(n)​G¯,superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛𝐺¯superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛𝐺\sigma_{k}^{t}(-n)G=\overline{\sigma_{k}^{t}(n)G},

with a gauge factor G𝐺G which is given below in eq.(11). In order to simplify the final expression, let us parametrise by

|a|2=ϵ​r−2+1/r4,b=2​a1−r​1−r​c1+c,pi​1=1−1/r2​a​1−r​pi1+pi,formulae-sequencesuperscript𝑎2italic-ϵ𝑟21𝑟4formulae-sequence𝑏2𝑎1𝑟1𝑟𝑐1𝑐subscript𝑝𝑖111𝑟2𝑎1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖|a|^{2}=\epsilon\frac{r-2+1/r}{4},\quad b=\frac{2a}{1-r}\frac{1-rc}{1+c},\quad p_{i1}=\frac{1-1/r}{2a}\frac{1-rp_{i}}{1+p_{i}},
ai​1=ϵ​r−1/r4​ai,ai​2=−a​(a¯+ϵ​pi​2)​bi,formulae-sequencesubscript𝑎𝑖1italic-ϵ𝑟1𝑟4subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖2𝑎¯𝑎italic-ϵsubscript𝑝𝑖2subscript𝑏𝑖a_{i1}=\epsilon\frac{r-1/r}{4}a_{i},\quad a_{i2}=-a(\bar{a}+\epsilon p_{i2})b_{i},\quad

where r𝑟r is a real constant, c𝑐c is a complex constant, pisubscript𝑝𝑖p_{i} represents the complex wave number of the i𝑖ith breather, aisubscript𝑎𝑖a_{i} and bisubscript𝑏𝑖b_{i} stand for the complex phase constants of the i𝑖ith breather. We comment that ϵitalic-ϵ\epsilon is equal to the sign of r𝑟r because of the positivity of |a|2superscript𝑎2|a|^{2}. Then we have

m~i​jn​(k,K,l,L)superscriptsubscript~𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿\displaystyle\tilde{m}_{ij}^{n}(k,K,l,L)
:=mi​jn​(k,K,l,L)​−4(1−r)​(1+pi)​(1+p¯j)​(−r​1−p¯j21−r2​p¯j2)n​rK−k​(r​c)−l​(r​c¯​1−pi2/c¯21−r2​pi2​1−p¯j21−r2​c¯2​p¯j2)Lassignabsentsuperscriptsubscript𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝐾𝑙𝐿41𝑟1subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗superscript𝑟1superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑗21superscript𝑟2superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑗2𝑛superscript𝑟𝐾𝑘superscript𝑟𝑐𝑙superscript𝑟¯𝑐1superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2superscript¯𝑐21superscript𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖21superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑗21superscript𝑟2superscript¯𝑐2superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑗2𝐿\displaystyle:=m_{ij}^{n}(k,K,l,L)\frac{-4}{(1-r)(1+p_{i})(1+\bar{p}_{j})}\left(-r\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}^{2}}{1-r^{2}\bar{p}_{j}^{2}}\right)^{n}r^{K-k}(rc)^{-l}\left(r\bar{c}\frac{1-p_{i}^{2}/\bar{c}^{2}}{1-r^{2}p_{i}^{2}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}^{2}}{1-r^{2}\bar{c}^{2}\bar{p}_{j}^{2}}\right)^{L}
=ai​a¯j1−r​pi​p¯j​(1−r​pi1+pi​1+p¯j1−r​p¯j)n​(1+pi1+r​pi​1+p¯j1+r​p¯j)k​(1−r​pi1−pi​1−r​p¯j1−p¯j)Kabsentsubscript𝑎𝑖subscript¯𝑎𝑗1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑛superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑘superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐾\displaystyle=\frac{a_{i}\bar{a}_{j}}{1-rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}\left(\frac{1-rp_{i}}{1+p_{i}}\frac{1+\bar{p}_{j}}{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{n}\left(\frac{1+p_{i}}{1+rp_{i}}\frac{1+\bar{p}_{j}}{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{k}\left(\frac{1-rp_{i}}{1-p_{i}}\frac{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}{1-\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{K}
×(1+pi1+r​c​pi​1+p¯j/c1+r​p¯j)l​(1+pi/c¯1+r​pi​1+p¯j1+r​c¯​p¯j)Labsentsuperscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟𝑐subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑐1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑙superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖¯𝑐1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟¯𝑐subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐿\displaystyle\hskip 50.0pt\times\left(\frac{1+p_{i}}{1+rcp_{i}}\frac{1+\bar{p}_{j}/c}{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{l}\left(\frac{1+p_{i}/\bar{c}}{1+rp_{i}}\frac{1+\bar{p}_{j}}{1+r\bar{c}\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{L}
−ai​b¯j1+r​pi​p¯j​(1−r​pi1+pi​1−p¯j1+r​p¯j)n​(1+pi1+r​pi​1−p¯j1−r​p¯j)k​(1−r​pi1−pi​1+r​p¯j1+p¯j)Ksubscript𝑎𝑖subscript¯𝑏𝑗1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑛superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑘superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐾\displaystyle-\frac{a_{i}\bar{b}_{j}}{1+rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}\left(\frac{1-rp_{i}}{1+p_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}}{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{n}\left(\frac{1+p_{i}}{1+rp_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}}{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{k}\left(\frac{1-rp_{i}}{1-p_{i}}\frac{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}{1+\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{K}
×(1+pi1+r​c​pi​1−p¯j/c1−r​p¯j)l​(1+pi/c¯1+r​pi​1−p¯j1−r​c¯​p¯j)Labsentsuperscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟𝑐subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑐1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑙superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖¯𝑐1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟¯𝑐subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐿\displaystyle\hskip 50.0pt\times\left(\frac{1+p_{i}}{1+rcp_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}/c}{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{l}\left(\frac{1+p_{i}/\bar{c}}{1+rp_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}}{1-r\bar{c}\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{L}
−bi​a¯j1+r​pi​p¯j​(1+r​pi1−pi​1+p¯j1−r​p¯j)n​(1−pi1−r​pi​1+p¯j1+r​p¯j)k​(1+r​pi1+pi​1−r​p¯j1−p¯j)Ksubscript𝑏𝑖subscript¯𝑎𝑗1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑛superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑘superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐾\displaystyle-\frac{b_{i}\bar{a}_{j}}{1+rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}\left(\frac{1+rp_{i}}{1-p_{i}}\frac{1+\bar{p}_{j}}{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{n}\left(\frac{1-p_{i}}{1-rp_{i}}\frac{1+\bar{p}_{j}}{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{k}\left(\frac{1+rp_{i}}{1+p_{i}}\frac{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}{1-\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{K}

+bi​b¯j1−r​pi​p¯j​(1+r​pi1−pi​1−p¯j1+r​p¯j)n​(1−pi1−r​pi​1−p¯j1−r​p¯j)k​(1+r​pi1+pi​1+r​p¯j1+p¯j)Ksubscript𝑏𝑖subscript¯𝑏𝑗1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑛superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑘superscript1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐾\displaystyle+\frac{b_{i}\bar{b}_{j}}{1-rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}\left(\frac{1+rp_{i}}{1-p_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}}{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{n}\left(\frac{1-p_{i}}{1-rp_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}}{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{k}\left(\frac{1+rp_{i}}{1+p_{i}}\frac{1+r\bar{p}_{j}}{1+\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{K}
×(1−pi1−r​c​pi​1−p¯j/c1−r​p¯j)l​(1−pi/c¯1−r​pi​1−p¯j1−r​c¯​p¯j)L,absentsuperscript1subscript𝑝𝑖1𝑟𝑐subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑐1𝑟subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝑙superscript1subscript𝑝𝑖¯𝑐1𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑗1𝑟¯𝑐subscript¯𝑝𝑗𝐿\displaystyle\hskip 50.0pt\times\left(\frac{1-p_{i}}{1-rcp_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}/c}{1-r\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{l}\left(\frac{1-p_{i}/\bar{c}}{1-rp_{i}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{j}}{1-r\bar{c}\bar{p}_{j}}\right)^{L},

and the bilinear equations (9) are reduced to

{(1+r)2​τk+1t​(n)​τk−1t​(n)−4​r​τkt​(n)​τkt​(n)−(1−r)2​τkt​(n+1)​τkt​(n−1)=0,(1−c)​(1+r​c¯)​τkt+1​(n+1)​τkt​(n)−(1−c¯)​(1+r​c)​τkt​(n+1)​τkt+1​(n)−(1+c¯)​(1−r​c)​τk−1t+1​(n+1)​τk+1t​(n)+(1+c)​(1−r​c¯)​τk+1t​(n+1)​τk−1t+1​(n)=0,casessuperscript1𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘1𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘1𝑡𝑛4𝑟superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛superscript1𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛101𝑐1𝑟¯𝑐superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡1𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛1¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡1𝑛1¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘1𝑡1𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘1𝑡𝑛1𝑐1𝑟¯𝑐superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘1𝑡𝑛1superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘1𝑡1𝑛0\left\{\begin{array}[]{l}(1+r)^{2}\tau_{k+1}^{t}(n)\tau_{k-1}^{t}(n)-4r\tau_{k}^{t}(n)\tau_{k}^{t}(n)-(1-r)^{2}\tau_{k}^{t}(n+1)\tau_{k}^{t}(n-1)=0,\\[5.0pt] (1-c)(1+r\bar{c})\tau_{k}^{t+1}(n+1)\tau_{k}^{t}(n)-(1-\bar{c})(1+rc)\tau_{k}^{t}(n+1)\tau_{k}^{t+1}(n)\\[5.0pt] \quad-(1+\bar{c})(1-rc)\tau_{k-1}^{t+1}(n+1)\tau_{k+1}^{t}(n)+(1+c)(1-r\bar{c})\tau_{k+1}^{t}(n+1)\tau_{k-1}^{t+1}(n)=0,\end{array}\right. (10)

where τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n) is given by

τkt​(n)=det1≤i,j≤N(m~i​jn​(k,0,t,t))=σkt​(n)​G,superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛subscriptformulae-sequence1𝑖𝑗𝑁superscriptsubscript~𝑚𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘0𝑡𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑘𝑡𝑛𝐺\tau_{k}^{t}(n)=\det_{1\leq i,j\leq N}\left(\tilde{m}_{ij}^{n}(k,0,t,t)\right)=\sigma_{k}^{t}(n)G,
G=∏i=1N−4(1−r)​(1+pi)​(1+p¯i)​(−r​1−p¯i21−r2​p¯i2)n​r−k​(c¯c​1−pi2/c¯21−r2​pi2​1−p¯i21−r2​c¯2​p¯i2)t,𝐺superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑖1𝑁41𝑟1subscript𝑝𝑖1subscript¯𝑝𝑖superscript𝑟1superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑖21superscript𝑟2superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑖2𝑛superscript𝑟𝑘superscript¯𝑐𝑐1superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2superscript¯𝑐21superscript𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖21superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑖21superscript𝑟2superscript¯𝑐2superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑖2𝑡G=\prod_{i=1}^{N}\frac{-4}{(1-r)(1+p_{i})(1+\bar{p}_{i})}\left(-r\frac{1-\bar{p}_{i}^{2}}{1-r^{2}\bar{p}_{i}^{2}}\right)^{n}r^{-k}\left(\frac{\bar{c}}{c}\frac{1-p_{i}^{2}/\bar{c}^{2}}{1-r^{2}p_{i}^{2}}\frac{1-\bar{p}_{i}^{2}}{1-r^{2}\bar{c}^{2}\bar{p}_{i}^{2}}\right)^{t}, (11)

which is nothing but τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n) in Theorem 1. For fkt=τkt​(0)superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡0f_{k}^{t}=\tau_{k}^{t}(0), gkt=τkt​(1)superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡1g_{k}^{t}=\tau_{k}^{t}(1), g¯kt=τkt​(−1)superscriptsubscript¯𝑔𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡1\bar{g}_{k}^{t}=\tau_{k}^{t}(-1), the bilinear equations are written as

{(1+r)2​fk+1t​fk−1t−4​r​fkt​fkt−(1−r)2​gkt​g¯kt=0,(1−c)​(1+r​c¯)​gkt+1​fkt−(1−c¯)​(1+r​c)​gkt​fkt+1−(1+c¯)​(1−r​c)​gk−1t+1​fk+1t+(1+c)​(1−r​c¯)​gk+1t​fk−1t+1=0,casessuperscript1𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘1𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘1𝑡4𝑟superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡superscript1𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript¯𝑔𝑘𝑡01𝑐1𝑟¯𝑐superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡1¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡11¯𝑐1𝑟𝑐superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘1𝑡1superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘1𝑡1𝑐1𝑟¯𝑐superscriptsubscript𝑔𝑘1𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘1𝑡10\left\{\begin{array}[]{l}(1+r)^{2}f_{k+1}^{t}f_{k-1}^{t}-4rf_{k}^{t}f_{k}^{t}-(1-r)^{2}g_{k}^{t}\bar{g}_{k}^{t}=0,\\[5.0pt] (1-c)(1+r\bar{c})g_{k}^{t+1}f_{k}^{t}-(1-\bar{c})(1+rc)g_{k}^{t}f_{k}^{t+1}\\[5.0pt] \quad-(1+\bar{c})(1-rc)g_{k-1}^{t+1}f_{k+1}^{t}+(1+c)(1-r\bar{c})g_{k+1}^{t}f_{k-1}^{t+1}=0,\end{array}\right.

from which the fully discrete NLS eq.(1) is straightforwardly derived through the variable transformation (2). This completes the proof of Theorem 1. ∎

In the case of N=1𝑁1N=1, we have the 1-breather solution,

τkt​(n)=11−r​|p|2​(|a|2​φn​(p)​φ−n​(p)¯+|b|2​φn​(−p)​φ−n​(−p)¯)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛11𝑟superscript𝑝2superscript𝑎2subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝¯subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝superscript𝑏2subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝¯subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝\displaystyle\tau_{k}^{t}(n)=\frac{1}{1-r|p|^{2}}\left(|a|^{2}\varphi_{n}(p)\overline{\varphi_{-n}(p)}+|b|^{2}\varphi_{n}(-p)\overline{\varphi_{-n}(-p)}\right)
−11+r​|p|2​(a​b¯​φn​(p)​φ−n​(−p)¯+a¯​b​φn​(−p)​φ−n​(p)¯),11𝑟superscript𝑝2𝑎¯𝑏subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝¯subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝¯𝑎𝑏subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝¯subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝\displaystyle\qquad-\frac{1}{1+r|p|^{2}}\left(a\bar{b}\varphi_{n}(p)\overline{\varphi_{-n}(-p)}+\bar{a}b\varphi_{n}(-p)\overline{\varphi_{-n}(p)}\right),

with (2)-(7) where p𝑝p, a𝑎a, b𝑏b are complex constants (the index 111 of p1subscript𝑝1p_{1}, a1subscript𝑎1a_{1}, b1subscript𝑏1b_{1} are omitted for notational simplicity). The Akhmediev breather which is the breather solution localized in time t𝑡t can be derived by taking the wave number p𝑝p pure imaginary. If we require the regularity of the solution only on the lattice points, i.e., fkt≠0superscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡0f_{k}^{t}\neq 0 for integers k𝑘k and t𝑡t, then r𝑟r can be either positive or negative and the discrete NLS equations (1) of both focusing type and defocusing type admit regular breather solutions by locating the singularities (if exist) off the lattice points. On the other hand if we require the solution to be regular in whole real space, i.e., fktsuperscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡f_{k}^{t} is non-zero for all real numbers k𝑘k and t𝑡t, then r𝑟r has to be positive and only the focusing discrete NLS admits the regular breathers for generic parameters. Some exceptional regular solutions for r<0𝑟0r<0 can be derived from the above τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n) but usually they are not called breathers and we don’t discuss about such solutions.

3 Rogue wave solution for fully discrete NLS equation

The rogue wave solution of rational function type can be derived as a limit of the breather solution. In Theorem 1, we take

ai=12​(1+∑ν=1icν​pi2​ν−1),bi=12​(1−∑ν=1icν​pi2​ν−1),formulae-sequencesubscript𝑎𝑖121superscriptsubscript𝜈1𝑖subscript𝑐𝜈superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2𝜈1subscript𝑏𝑖121superscriptsubscript𝜈1𝑖subscript𝑐𝜈superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2𝜈1a_{i}=\frac{1}{2}\Big{(}1+\sum_{\nu=1}^{i}c_{\nu}p_{i}^{2\nu-1}\Big{)},\quad b_{i}=\frac{1}{2}\Big{(}1-\sum_{\nu=1}^{i}c_{\nu}p_{i}^{2\nu-1}\Big{)},

scale the τ𝜏\tau function by τkt​(n)/∏i=1N(pi​p¯i)2​i−1superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscriptproduct𝑖1𝑁superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑖2𝑖1\displaystyle\tau_{k}^{t}(n)/\prod_{i=1}^{N}(p_{i}\bar{p}_{i})^{2i-1} and finally take the limit pi→0→subscript𝑝𝑖0p_{i}\to 0 successively for i=1,2,…,N𝑖12…𝑁i=1,2,\ldots,N. Then the leading order of τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n) in pisubscript𝑝𝑖p_{i}’s gives a polynomial of k𝑘k and t𝑡t which turns to be the rogue wave solution. This result is summarized in the following theorem.

Theorem 2.

The N𝑁Nth order rogue wave solution for the fully discrete NLS equation (1) is given by

τkt​(n)=det1≤i,j≤N(Bi​j(n)​(k,t)),Bi​j(n)=∑ν=12​min⁡(i,j)rν−1​Φ2​i−ν(n)​Φ2​j−ν(−n)¯,formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛subscriptformulae-sequence1𝑖𝑗𝑁superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝑡superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑖𝑗𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜈12𝑖𝑗superscript𝑟𝜈1superscriptsubscriptΦ2𝑖𝜈𝑛¯superscriptsubscriptΦ2𝑗𝜈𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n)=\det_{1\leq i,j\leq N}\left(B_{ij}^{(n)}(k,t)\right),\qquad B_{ij}^{(n)}=\sum_{\nu=1}^{2\min(i,j)}r^{\nu-1}\Phi_{2i-\nu}^{(n)}\overline{\Phi_{2j-\nu}^{(-n)}},
Φi(n)=Si​(x​(n))+∑μ=1[i+12]cμ​Si+1−2​μ​(x​(n)),x​(n)=(x1​(n),x2​(n),…,xh​(n),…),formulae-sequencesuperscriptsubscriptΦ𝑖𝑛subscript𝑆𝑖𝑥𝑛superscriptsubscript𝜇1delimited-[]𝑖12subscript𝑐𝜇subscript𝑆𝑖12𝜇𝑥𝑛𝑥𝑛subscript𝑥1𝑛subscript𝑥2𝑛…subscript𝑥ℎ𝑛…\Phi_{i}^{(n)}=S_{i}(x(n))+\sum_{\mu=1}^{\left[\frac{i+1}{2}\right]}c_{\mu}S_{i+1-2\mu}(x(n)),\qquad x(n)=(x_{1}(n),x_{2}(n),\ldots,x_{h}(n),\ldots),
xh​(n)=(−1)hh​((1−(−r)h)​n−(1−rh)​k−(1−rh+(1/c¯)h−(r​c)h)​t),for ​h=1,2,…,formulae-sequencesubscript𝑥ℎ𝑛superscript1ℎℎ1superscript𝑟ℎ𝑛1superscript𝑟ℎ𝑘1superscript𝑟ℎsuperscript1¯𝑐ℎsuperscript𝑟𝑐ℎ𝑡for ℎ12…x_{h}(n)=\frac{(-1)^{h}}{h}\left((1-(-r)^{h})n-(1-r^{h})k-(1-r^{h}+(1/\bar{c})^{h}-(rc)^{h})t\right),\quad\hbox{for }h=1,2,\ldots,

with the variable transformations (2)-(4), where [][\ ] means the Gauss symbol and Sμ​(x)subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥S_{\mu}(x) is the so-called elementary Schur function defined by ∑μ=0∞Sμ​(x)​λμ=exp​∑h=1∞xh​λhsuperscriptsubscript𝜇0subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥superscript𝜆𝜇superscriptsubscriptℎ1subscript𝑥ℎsuperscript𝜆ℎ\displaystyle\sum_{\mu=0}^{\infty}S_{\mu}(x)\lambda^{\mu}=\exp\sum_{h=1}^{\infty}x_{h}\lambda^{h}. This solution has N𝑁N complex parameters cμsubscript𝑐𝜇c_{\mu}, μ=1,2,…,N𝜇12…𝑁\mu=1,2,\ldots,N.

Proof.

Firstly φn​(p)subscript𝜑𝑛𝑝\varphi_{n}(p) in (7) is written as φn​(p)=∑μ=0∞Sμ​(x​(n))​pμsubscript𝜑𝑛𝑝superscriptsubscript𝜇0subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥𝑛superscript𝑝𝜇\displaystyle\varphi_{n}(p)=\sum_{\mu=0}^{\infty}S_{\mu}(x(n))p^{\mu}. We rewrite Ai​j(n)superscriptsubscript𝐴𝑖𝑗𝑛A_{ij}^{(n)} in Theorem 1 as

Ai​j(n)=11−(r​pi​p¯j)2​(ai​φn​(pi)−bi​φn​(−pi))​(aj​φ−n​(pj)−bj​φ−n​(−pj)¯)superscriptsubscript𝐴𝑖𝑗𝑛11superscript𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗2subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖¯subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗subscript𝑏𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗\displaystyle A_{ij}^{(n)}=\frac{1}{1-(rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j})^{2}}\Big{(}a_{i}\varphi_{n}(p_{i})-b_{i}\varphi_{n}(-p_{i})\Big{)}\Big{(}\overline{a_{j}\varphi_{-n}(p_{j})-b_{j}\varphi_{-n}(-p_{j})}\Big{)}
+r​pi​p¯j1−(r​pi​p¯j)2​(ai​φn​(pi)+bi​φn​(−pi))​(aj​φ−n​(pj)+bj​φ−n​(−pj)¯).𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗1superscript𝑟subscript𝑝𝑖subscript¯𝑝𝑗2subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖¯subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗subscript𝑏𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗\displaystyle\qquad+\frac{rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j}}{1-(rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j})^{2}}\Big{(}a_{i}\varphi_{n}(p_{i})+b_{i}\varphi_{n}(-p_{i})\Big{)}\Big{(}\overline{a_{j}\varphi_{-n}(p_{j})+b_{j}\varphi_{-n}(-p_{j})}\Big{)}.

Denoting ai−bi=pi​disubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑑𝑖a_{i}-b_{i}=p_{i}d_{i} and ai+bi=sisubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑠𝑖a_{i}+b_{i}=s_{i}, the four factors in the above expression are written as

ai​φn​(pi)−bi​φn​(−pi)=∑μ=0∞Sμ​(x​(n))​piμ​(ai−(−1)μ​bi)subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜇0subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖𝜇subscript𝑎𝑖superscript1𝜇subscript𝑏𝑖\displaystyle a_{i}\varphi_{n}(p_{i})-b_{i}\varphi_{n}(-p_{i})=\sum_{\mu=0}^{\infty}S_{\mu}(x(n))p_{i}^{\mu}(a_{i}-(-1)^{\mu}b_{i})
=(pidi,pisi,pi3di,pi3si,⋯)(S0(x(n)),S1(x(n)),S2(x(n)),S3(x(n)),⋯)t,\displaystyle\qquad=(p_{i}d_{i},p_{i}s_{i},p_{i}^{3}d_{i},p_{i}^{3}s_{i},\cdots)\,{}^{t}(S_{0}(x(n)),S_{1}(x(n)),S_{2}(x(n)),S_{3}(x(n)),\cdots),
aj​φ−n​(pj)−bj​φ−n​(−pj)¯=∑μ=0∞Sμ​(x​(−n))​pjμ​(aj−(−1)μ​bj)¯¯subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗subscript𝑏𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜇0¯subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑗𝜇subscript𝑎𝑗superscript1𝜇subscript𝑏𝑗\displaystyle\overline{a_{j}\varphi_{-n}(p_{j})-b_{j}\varphi_{-n}(-p_{j})}=\sum_{\mu=0}^{\infty}\overline{S_{\mu}(x(-n))p_{j}^{\mu}(a_{j}-(-1)^{\mu}b_{j})}
=(S0(x​(−n)¯),S1(x​(−n)¯),S2(x​(−n)¯),S3(x​(−n)¯),⋯)(p¯jd¯j,p¯js¯j,p¯j3d¯j,p¯j3s¯j,⋯)t,\displaystyle\qquad=(S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)}),\cdots)\,{}^{t}(\bar{p}_{j}\bar{d}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}\bar{s}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{3}\bar{d}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{3}\bar{s}_{j},\cdots),
ai​φn​(pi)+bi​φn​(−pi)=∑μ=0∞Sμ​(x​(n))​piμ​(ai+(−1)μ​bi)subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜇0subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖𝜇subscript𝑎𝑖superscript1𝜇subscript𝑏𝑖\displaystyle a_{i}\varphi_{n}(p_{i})+b_{i}\varphi_{n}(-p_{i})=\sum_{\mu=0}^{\infty}S_{\mu}(x(n))p_{i}^{\mu}(a_{i}+(-1)^{\mu}b_{i})
=(si,pi2di,pi2si,pi4di,⋯)(S0(x(n)),S1(x(n)),S2(x(n)),S3(x(n)),⋯)t,\displaystyle\qquad=(s_{i},p_{i}^{2}d_{i},p_{i}^{2}s_{i},p_{i}^{4}d_{i},\cdots)\,{}^{t}(S_{0}(x(n)),S_{1}(x(n)),S_{2}(x(n)),S_{3}(x(n)),\cdots),
aj​φ−n​(pj)+bj​φ−n​(−pj)¯=∑μ=0∞Sμ​(x​(−n))​pjμ​(aj+(−1)μ​bj)¯¯subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗subscript𝑏𝑗subscript𝜑𝑛subscript𝑝𝑗superscriptsubscript𝜇0¯subscript𝑆𝜇𝑥𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑗𝜇subscript𝑎𝑗superscript1𝜇subscript𝑏𝑗\displaystyle\overline{a_{j}\varphi_{-n}(p_{j})+b_{j}\varphi_{-n}(-p_{j})}=\sum_{\mu=0}^{\infty}\overline{S_{\mu}(x(-n))p_{j}^{\mu}(a_{j}+(-1)^{\mu}b_{j})}
=(S0(x​(−n)¯),S1(x​(−n)¯),S2(x​(−n)¯),S3(x​(−n)¯),⋯)(s¯j,p¯j2d¯j,p¯j2s¯j,p¯j4d¯j,⋯)t,\displaystyle\qquad=(S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)}),\cdots)\,{}^{t}(\bar{s}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{2}\bar{d}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{2}\bar{s}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{4}\bar{d}_{j},\cdots),

where vtsuperscript𝑣𝑡{}^{t}v means transpose of v𝑣v. Thus we obtain

Ai​j(n)=∑λ=0∞(rpip¯j)2​λ(pidi,pisi,pi3di,pi3si,⋯)(S0(x(n)),S1(x(n)),S2(x(n)),S3(x(n)),⋯)t\displaystyle A_{ij}^{(n)}=\sum_{\lambda=0}^{\infty}(rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j})^{2\lambda}(p_{i}d_{i},p_{i}s_{i},p_{i}^{3}d_{i},p_{i}^{3}s_{i},\cdots)\,{}^{t}(S_{0}(x(n)),S_{1}(x(n)),S_{2}(x(n)),S_{3}(x(n)),\cdots)
×(S0(x​(−n)¯),S1(x​(−n)¯),S2(x​(−n)¯),S3(x​(−n)¯),⋯)(p¯jd¯j,p¯js¯j,p¯j3d¯j,p¯j3s¯j,⋯)t\displaystyle\hskip 40.0pt\times(S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)}),\cdots)\,{}^{t}(\bar{p}_{j}\bar{d}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}\bar{s}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{3}\bar{d}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{3}\bar{s}_{j},\cdots)
+∑λ=0∞(rpip¯j)2​λ+1(si,pi2di,pi2si,pi4di,⋯)(S0(x(n)),S1(x(n)),S2(x(n)),S3(x(n)),⋯)t\displaystyle\qquad+\sum_{\lambda=0}^{\infty}(rp_{i}\bar{p}_{j})^{2\lambda+1}(s_{i},p_{i}^{2}d_{i},p_{i}^{2}s_{i},p_{i}^{4}d_{i},\cdots)\,{}^{t}(S_{0}(x(n)),S_{1}(x(n)),S_{2}(x(n)),S_{3}(x(n)),\cdots)
×(S0(x​(−n)¯),S1(x​(−n)¯),S2(x​(−n)¯),S3(x​(−n)¯),⋯)(s¯j,p¯j2d¯j,p¯j2s¯j,p¯j4d¯j,⋯)t\displaystyle\hskip 40.0pt\times(S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)}),S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)}),\cdots)\,{}^{t}(\bar{s}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{2}\bar{d}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{2}\bar{s}_{j},\bar{p}_{j}^{4}\bar{d}_{j},\cdots)
=(pi​dipi​sipi3​dipi3​si⋯)​(S0​(x​(n))0S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))S2​(x​(n))S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))S3​(x​(n))S2​(x​(n))S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))⋮⋮⋮⋱)absentmatrixsubscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑑𝑖subscript𝑝𝑖subscript𝑠𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖3subscript𝑑𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖3subscript𝑠𝑖⋯matrixsubscript𝑆0𝑥𝑛missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression0subscript𝑆1𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆0𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆0𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆3𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆0𝑥𝑛⋮⋮⋮missing-subexpression⋱\displaystyle\qquad=\begin{pmatrix}p_{i}d_{i}&p_{i}s_{i}&p_{i}^{3}d_{i}&p_{i}^{3}s_{i}&\cdots\end{pmatrix}\raisebox{-30.0pt}{$\begin{pmatrix}S_{0}(x(n))&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ S_{2}(x(n))&S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ S_{3}(x(n))&S_{2}(x(n))&S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ \vdots&\vdots&\vdots&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}$}
×(10rr2r30⋱)​(S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)S2​(x​(−n)¯)S3​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)S2​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)0⋱)​(p¯j​d¯jp¯j​s¯jp¯j3​d¯jp¯j3​s¯j⋮).absentmatrix1missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression0missing-subexpression𝑟missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsuperscript𝑟2missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsuperscript𝑟30missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression⋱matrixsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆3¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛0missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression⋱matrixsubscript¯𝑝𝑗subscript¯𝑑𝑗subscript¯𝑝𝑗subscript¯𝑠𝑗superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑗3subscript¯𝑑𝑗superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑗3subscript¯𝑠𝑗⋮\displaystyle\qquad\times\begin{pmatrix}1&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ &r\\ &&r^{2}\\ &&&r^{3}\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &&S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &&&S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}\bar{p}_{j}\bar{d}_{j}\\ \bar{p}_{j}\bar{s}_{j}\\ \bar{p}_{j}^{3}\bar{d}_{j}\\ \bar{p}_{j}^{3}\bar{s}_{j}\\ \vdots\end{pmatrix}.

Therefore τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n) in Theorem 1 is given in the form of the following determinant,

τkt(n)=|(p1​d1p1​s1p13​d1p13​s1⋯p2​d2p2​s2p23​d2p23​s2⋯⋮⋮⋮⋮pN​dNpN​sNpN3​dNpN3​sN⋯)(S0​(x​(n))0S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))S2​(x​(n))S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))S3​(x​(n))S2​(x​(n))S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))⋮⋮⋮⋱)\displaystyle\tau_{k}^{t}(n)=\left|\raisebox{8.0pt}{$\begin{pmatrix}p_{1}d_{1}&p_{1}s_{1}&p_{1}^{3}d_{1}&p_{1}^{3}s_{1}&\cdots\\ p_{2}d_{2}&p_{2}s_{2}&p_{2}^{3}d_{2}&p_{2}^{3}s_{2}&\cdots\\ \vdots&\vdots&\vdots&\vdots\\ p_{N}d_{N}&p_{N}s_{N}&p_{N}^{3}d_{N}&p_{N}^{3}s_{N}&\cdots\end{pmatrix}$}\begin{pmatrix}S_{0}(x(n))&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ S_{2}(x(n))&S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ S_{3}(x(n))&S_{2}(x(n))&S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ \vdots&\vdots&\vdots&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}\right.
×(10rr2r30⋱)​(S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)S2​(x​(−n)¯)S3​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)S2​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)0⋱)absentmatrix1missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression0missing-subexpression𝑟missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsuperscript𝑟2missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsuperscript𝑟30missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression⋱matrixsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆3¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛0missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression⋱\displaystyle\hskip 40.0pt\times\begin{pmatrix}1&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ &r\\ &&r^{2}\\ &&&r^{3}\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &&S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &&&S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}
×(p¯1​d¯1p¯2​d¯2⋯p¯N​d¯Np¯1​s¯1p¯2​s¯2⋯p¯N​s¯Np¯13​d¯1p¯23​d¯2⋯p¯N3​d¯Np¯13​s¯1p¯23​s¯2⋯p¯N3​s¯N⋮⋮⋮)|.\displaystyle\hskip 40.0pt\times\left.\begin{pmatrix}\bar{p}_{1}\bar{d}_{1}&\bar{p}_{2}\bar{d}_{2}&\cdots&\bar{p}_{N}\bar{d}_{N}\\ \bar{p}_{1}\bar{s}_{1}&\bar{p}_{2}\bar{s}_{2}&\cdots&\bar{p}_{N}\bar{s}_{N}\\ \bar{p}_{1}^{3}\bar{d}_{1}&\bar{p}_{2}^{3}\bar{d}_{2}&\cdots&\bar{p}_{N}^{3}\bar{d}_{N}\\ \bar{p}_{1}^{3}\bar{s}_{1}&\bar{p}_{2}^{3}\bar{s}_{2}&\cdots&\bar{p}_{N}^{3}\bar{s}_{N}\\ \vdots&\vdots&&\vdots\\ \end{pmatrix}\right|.

Now let us take

ai=12​(1+∑ν=1icν​pi2​ν−1),bi=12​(1−∑ν=1icν​pi2​ν−1).formulae-sequencesubscript𝑎𝑖121superscriptsubscript𝜈1𝑖subscript𝑐𝜈superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2𝜈1subscript𝑏𝑖121superscriptsubscript𝜈1𝑖subscript𝑐𝜈superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2𝜈1a_{i}=\frac{1}{2}\Big{(}1+\sum_{\nu=1}^{i}c_{\nu}p_{i}^{2\nu-1}\Big{)},\quad b_{i}=\frac{1}{2}\Big{(}1-\sum_{\nu=1}^{i}c_{\nu}p_{i}^{2\nu-1}\Big{)}.

Then we have di=∑ν=1icν​pi2​ν−2subscript𝑑𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜈1𝑖subscript𝑐𝜈superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑖2𝜈2\displaystyle d_{i}=\sum_{\nu=1}^{i}c_{\nu}p_{i}^{2\nu-2} and si=1subscript𝑠𝑖1s_{i}=1, and the above τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n) is O​(p1​p¯1​p2​p¯2​⋯​pN​p¯N)𝑂subscript𝑝1subscript¯𝑝1subscript𝑝2subscript¯𝑝2⋯subscript𝑝𝑁subscript¯𝑝𝑁O(p_{1}\bar{p}_{1}p_{2}\bar{p}_{2}\cdots p_{N}\bar{p}_{N}) as pi→0→subscript𝑝𝑖0p_{i}\to 0 for 1≤i≤N1𝑖𝑁1\leq i\leq N. In order to take the lowest order in p1subscript𝑝1p_{1}, we consider the limit, τ~kt​(n):=limp1→0τkt​(n)/(p1​p¯1)assignsuperscriptsubscript~𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛subscript→subscript𝑝10superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛subscript𝑝1subscript¯𝑝1\displaystyle\tilde{\tau}_{k}^{t}(n):=\lim_{p_{1}\to 0}\tau_{k}^{t}(n)/(p_{1}\bar{p}_{1}). In this limit, the leading order becomes O​((p2​p¯2​⋯​pN​p¯N)3)𝑂superscriptsubscript𝑝2subscript¯𝑝2⋯subscript𝑝𝑁subscript¯𝑝𝑁3O((p_{2}\bar{p}_{2}\cdots p_{N}\bar{p}_{N})^{3}), thus for picking up the lowest order in p2subscript𝑝2p_{2}, we take the limit, limp2→0τ~kt​(n)/(p2​p¯2)3subscript→subscript𝑝20superscriptsubscript~𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑝2subscript¯𝑝23\displaystyle\lim_{p_{2}\to 0}\tilde{\tau}_{k}^{t}(n)/(p_{2}\bar{p}_{2})^{3}. So the leading order becomes O​((p3​p¯3​⋯​pN​p¯N)5)𝑂superscriptsubscript𝑝3subscript¯𝑝3⋯subscript𝑝𝑁subscript¯𝑝𝑁5O((p_{3}\bar{p}_{3}\cdots p_{N}\bar{p}_{N})^{5}). Repeating this procedure, finally we obtain the τ𝜏\tau function of rogue wave solution from that of breather, τkt​(n)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛\tau_{k}^{t}(n),

limpN→0⋯​limp2→0limp1→0τkt​(n)p1​p¯1​p23​p¯23​⋯​pN2​N−1​p¯N2​N−1subscript→subscript𝑝𝑁0⋯subscript→subscript𝑝20subscript→subscript𝑝10superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛subscript𝑝1subscript¯𝑝1superscriptsubscript𝑝23superscriptsubscript¯𝑝23⋯superscriptsubscript𝑝𝑁2𝑁1superscriptsubscript¯𝑝𝑁2𝑁1\displaystyle\lim_{p_{N}\to 0}\cdots\lim_{p_{2}\to 0}\lim_{p_{1}\to 0}\frac{\tau_{k}^{t}(n)}{p_{1}\bar{p}_{1}p_{2}^{3}\bar{p}_{2}^{3}\cdots p_{N}^{2N-1}\bar{p}_{N}^{2N-1}}
=|(c110c20c11c30c20c11⋮⋮⋮⋮⋱cN0cN−10⋯⋯⋯c11)(S0​(x​(n))0S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))S2​(x​(n))S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))S3​(x​(n))S2​(x​(n))S1​(x​(n))S0​(x​(n))⋮⋮⋮⋱)\displaystyle=\left|\begin{pmatrix}c_{1}&1&&&&&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ c_{2}&0&c_{1}&1\\ c_{3}&0&c_{2}&0&c_{1}&1\\ \vdots&\vdots&\vdots&\vdots&&&\ddots\\ c_{N}&0&c_{N-1}&0&\cdots&\cdots&\cdots&c_{1}&1&\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}S_{0}(x(n))&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ S_{2}(x(n))&S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ S_{3}(x(n))&S_{2}(x(n))&S_{1}(x(n))&S_{0}(x(n))\\ \vdots&\vdots&\vdots&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}\right.
×(10rr2r30⋱)​(S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)S2​(x​(−n)¯)S3​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)S2​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)S1​(x​(−n)¯)⋯S0​(x​(−n)¯)0⋱)absentmatrix1missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression0missing-subexpression𝑟missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsuperscript𝑟2missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsuperscript𝑟30missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression⋱matrixsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆3¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆2¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛subscript𝑆1¯𝑥𝑛⋯missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionsubscript𝑆0¯𝑥𝑛0missing-subexpressionmissing-subexpressionmissing-subexpression⋱\displaystyle\hskip 30.0pt\times\begin{pmatrix}1&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ &r\\ &&r^{2}\\ &&&r^{3}\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{3}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{2}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &&S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})&S_{1}(\overline{x(-n)})&\cdots\\ &&&S_{0}(\overline{x(-n)})\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}
×(c¯1c¯2c¯3⋯c¯N100⋯0c¯1c¯2⋯c¯N−110⋯0c¯1⋮1⋮⋱⋮c¯110)|\displaystyle\hskip 30.0pt\times\left.\begin{pmatrix}\bar{c}_{1}&\bar{c}_{2}&\bar{c}_{3}&\cdots&\bar{c}_{N}\\ 1&0&0&\cdots&0\\ &\bar{c}_{1}&\bar{c}_{2}&\cdots&\bar{c}_{N-1}\\ &1&0&\cdots&0\\ &&\bar{c}_{1}&&\vdots\\ &&1&&\vdots\\ &&&\ddots&\vdots\\ &&&&\bar{c}_{1}\\ &&&&1\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}\end{pmatrix}\right|
=|(Φ1(n)Φ0(n)0Φ3(n)Φ2(n)Φ1(n)Φ0(n)Φ5(n)Φ4(n)Φ3(n)Φ2(n)Φ1(n)Φ0(n)⋮⋮⋮⋮⋱Φ2​N−1(n)Φ2​N−2(n)Φ2​N−3(n)Φ2​N−4(n)⋯⋯⋯Φ1(n)Φ0(n))(10rr2r30⋱)\displaystyle=\left|\begin{pmatrix}\Phi_{1}^{(n)}&\Phi_{0}^{(n)}&&&&&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ \Phi_{3}^{(n)}&\Phi_{2}^{(n)}&\Phi_{1}^{(n)}&\Phi_{0}^{(n)}\\ \Phi_{5}^{(n)}&\Phi_{4}^{(n)}&\Phi_{3}^{(n)}&\Phi_{2}^{(n)}&\Phi_{1}^{(n)}&\Phi_{0}^{(n)}\\ \vdots&\vdots&\vdots&\vdots&&&\ddots\\ \Phi_{2N-1}^{(n)}&\Phi_{2N-2}^{(n)}&\Phi_{2N-3}^{(n)}&\Phi_{2N-4}^{(n)}&\cdots&\cdots&\cdots&\Phi_{1}^{(n)}&\Phi_{0}^{(n)}\end{pmatrix}\right.\begin{pmatrix}1&&&&\raisebox{-5.0pt}{\smash{\Large 0}}\\ &r\\ &&r^{2}\\ &&&r^{3}\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}&&&&\ddots\end{pmatrix}
×(Φ1(−n)¯Φ3(−n)¯Φ5(−n)¯⋯Φ2​N−1(−n)¯Φ0(−n)¯Φ2(−n)¯Φ4(−n)¯⋯Φ2​N−2(−n)¯Φ1(−n)¯Φ3(−n)¯⋯Φ2​N−3(−n)¯Φ0(−n)¯Φ2(−n)¯⋯Φ2​N−4(−n)¯Φ1(−n)¯⋮Φ0(−n)¯⋮⋱⋮Φ1(−n)¯Φ0(−n)¯0)|.\displaystyle\hskip 30.0pt\times\left.\begin{pmatrix}\overline{\Phi_{1}^{(-n)}}&\overline{\Phi_{3}^{(-n)}}&\overline{\Phi_{5}^{(-n)}}&\cdots&\overline{\Phi_{2N-1}^{(-n)}}\\ \overline{\Phi_{0}^{(-n)}}&\overline{\Phi_{2}^{(-n)}}&\overline{\Phi_{4}^{(-n)}}&\cdots&\overline{\Phi_{2N-2}^{(-n)}}\\ &\overline{\Phi_{1}^{(-n)}}&\overline{\Phi_{3}^{(-n)}}&\cdots&\overline{\Phi_{2N-3}^{(-n)}}\\ &\overline{\Phi_{0}^{(-n)}}&\overline{\Phi_{2}^{(-n)}}&\cdots&\overline{\Phi_{2N-4}^{(-n)}}\\ &&\overline{\Phi_{1}^{(-n)}}&&\vdots\\ &&\overline{\Phi_{0}^{(-n)}}&&\vdots\\ &&&\ddots&\vdots\\ &&&&\overline{\Phi_{1}^{(-n)}}\\ &&&&\overline{\Phi_{0}^{(-n)}}\\ \mbox{\smash{\Large 0}}\end{pmatrix}\right|.

By calculating the matrix element, it is easy to see that the above determinant is equal to det1≤i,j≤N(Bi​j(n)​(k,t))subscriptformulae-sequence1𝑖𝑗𝑁superscriptsubscript𝐵𝑖𝑗𝑛𝑘𝑡\displaystyle\det_{1\leq i,j\leq N}\left(B_{ij}^{(n)}(k,t)\right). We completed the proof of Theorem 2. ∎

By taking N=1𝑁1N=1 we obtain the fully discrete Peregrine rogue wave solution,

τkt​(n)=(−(1+r)​n+(1−r)​k+(1−r+1/c¯−r​c)​t+c1)superscriptsubscript𝜏𝑘𝑡𝑛1𝑟𝑛1𝑟𝑘1𝑟1¯𝑐𝑟𝑐𝑡subscript𝑐1\displaystyle\tau_{k}^{t}(n)=\left(-(1+r)n+(1-r)k+(1-r+1/\bar{c}-rc)t+c_{1}\right)
×((1+r)​n+(1−r)​k+(1−r+1/c−r​c¯)​t+c¯1)+r,absent1𝑟𝑛1𝑟𝑘1𝑟1𝑐𝑟¯𝑐𝑡subscript¯𝑐1𝑟\displaystyle\hskip 30.0pt\times\left((1+r)n+(1-r)k+(1-r+1/c-r\bar{c})t+\bar{c}_{1}\right)+r, (12)

where c1subscript𝑐1c_{1} is a complex constant. Similarly to the breather solution, there are rogue wave solutions regular on the lattice for both focusing case (r>0𝑟0r>0) and defocusing case (r<0𝑟0r<0), since if there are zeros of fktsuperscriptsubscript𝑓𝑘𝑡f_{k}^{t} we can avoid explosion of solution by displacing the zeros off the lattice points. However for regularity of the solution on the real two dimensional space of (k,t)𝑘𝑡(k,t), we have to take r𝑟r positive. First order and second-order rogue wave solutions are shown in Figs. 2 and 3, respectively.

There is an exceptional regular solution for r<0𝑟0r<0 which is obtained by taking c𝑐c real and (Im​c1)2>−rsuperscriptImsubscript𝑐12𝑟({\rm Im}\,c_{1})^{2}>-r in (12), but this is not a rogue wave solution but a traveling wave solution. An example is shown in Fig. 4.

4 Concluding Remarks

Even though the study of rogue waves has attracted much attention in more than one decade, the rogue wave solution in fully discrete integrable systems has not been reported yet. In this paper, by taking the fully discrete NLS equation, we firstly constructed its general breather solution via the KP-Toda reduction method. Then by taking a list to the parameters succeeded in constructing its general rogue wave solution by taking the limit of pi→0→subscript𝑝𝑖0p_{i}\to 0 successively for i=1,⋯,N𝑖1⋯𝑁i=1,\cdots,N.

We expect to construct rogue wave solutions in other discrete systems such as the discrete complex sine-Gordon equation in the future.

Acknowledment

BF’s work is partially supported by National Science Foundation (NSF) under Grant No. DMS-1715991 and U.S. Department of Defense (DoD), Air Force for Scientific Research (AFOSR) under grant No. W911NF2010276.
